title: alangium villosum subsp. warburgianum
intro: Alangium villosum subsp. warburgianum is a plant species in the Alangiaceae family, commonly known as the alangium plant, characterized by its distinctive hairy stems and leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The alangium plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain and inflammation, and acting as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
